# Affitor Documentation

> Affitor is an affiliate growth platform for SaaS companies.
> This file provides LLM-readable documentation for integrating Affitor.
> Full docs: https://docs.affitor.com

> **AI coding agents — integration entry point:** follow https://docs.affitor.com/skill.md.
> It is a self-contained runbook for wiring click + lead + sale tracking and
> verifying the integration end-to-end (synthetic chain + readiness polling).

## Getting Started

- [What is Affitor?](https://docs.affitor.com/): Affiliate growth platform for SaaS with performance-based pricing and partner program management
- [How Affitor Works](https://docs.affitor.com/getting-started/how-it-works): The affiliate lifecycle from click to attributed revenue and payout operations
- [Pricing](https://docs.affitor.com/getting-started/pricing-performance-model): 3.5% platform fee on partner-generated revenue, no subscription, no setup fee

## For Advertisers

### Quickstart

- [Launch Your Program](https://docs.affitor.com/advertisers/quickstart): Overview of the advertiser setup flow
- [Create Your Advertiser Account](https://docs.affitor.com/advertisers/quickstart/create-account): Step-by-step guide to registering as an advertiser
- [Set Up Your Program](https://docs.affitor.com/advertisers/quickstart/setup-program): Configure your affiliate program and choose the right integration path
- [Partner Approval & Quality Control](https://docs.affitor.com/advertisers/quickstart/partner-approval-quality-control): Review applications and maintain program quality
- [View Performance](https://docs.affitor.com/advertisers/quickstart/view-performance): Monitor affiliate program metrics and partner results
- [Define Commission](https://docs.affitor.com/advertisers/quickstart/define-commission): Set up commission rates and structures for your program
- [Commission Approval & Cash Flow](https://docs.affitor.com/advertisers/quickstart/commission-approval-cash-flow): How attributed sales move into commissions, billing, and payout workflow
- [Payouts](https://docs.affitor.com/advertisers/quickstart/payouts): How partner commissions move through Affitor's payout workflow

### CLI

- [CLI Quickstart](https://docs.affitor.com/advertisers/cli/quickstart): Set up your affiliate program in 3 commands with npx affitor
- [CLI Command Reference](https://docs.affitor.com/advertisers/cli/commands): Complete reference for all npx affitor commands, flags, and options

### Tracking

- [3-Step Integration Guide](https://docs.affitor.com/advertisers/tracking/quickstart-integration): Get Affitor tracking live with the supported click, signup, and sale flows
- [Tracking Overview](https://docs.affitor.com/advertisers/tracking/tracking-overview): How Affitor links clicks, signups, and sales together for attribution
- [Pageview Tracker (Click)](https://docs.affitor.com/advertisers/tracking/pageview-tracker-click): Track affiliate visits with the Affitor tracker SDK or script tag
- [Lead Tracking (Signup)](https://docs.affitor.com/advertisers/tracking/lead-tracking-signup): Track referred signups with the browser helper or server-side lead API
- [Payment Tracking](https://docs.affitor.com/advertisers/tracking/payment-tracking-stripe): Track completed sales with Server-side tracking or Stripe metadata in Stripe integration
- [Payment Flow](https://docs.affitor.com/advertisers/tracking/payment-flow): End-to-end flow from affiliate click to attributed revenue and payout operations
- [Testing Integration](https://docs.affitor.com/advertisers/tracking/testing-integration): Verify click, signup, and sale tracking before going live

### Billing

- [Overdue Invoices & Program Pause Policy](https://docs.affitor.com/advertisers/billing/overdue-policy): How Affitor handles unpaid commission invoices, when programs pause, and how to resume

## For Partners

- [Become a Partner](https://docs.affitor.com/partners): Join a SaaS affiliate program, get your referral link, and start earning commission
- [Create Your Partner Account](https://docs.affitor.com/partners/quickstart/create-account): Sign up, verify your email, and choose the Partner role to unlock programs
- [Find and Join Programs](https://docs.affitor.com/partners/quickstart/find-and-join-programs): Browse the Marketplace, apply in one click, and get approved instantly or after review
- [Get Your Referral Link](https://docs.affitor.com/partners/quickstart/get-your-referral-link): Find, copy, and share your tracked ?aff= referral link, generated automatically when you join
- [Your Partner Dashboard](https://docs.affitor.com/partners/dashboard): Programs, performance, referred customers, and earnings
- [Getting Paid](https://docs.affitor.com/partners/payouts): Balances, payment methods (ACH/BIC-SWIFT/Wise), withdrawal, and payout timing
- [Partner FAQ](https://docs.affitor.com/partners/faq): Common partner questions about joining, links, tracking, and payouts

## API Reference

- [API Reference Overview](https://docs.affitor.com/api-reference/overview): Base URL, authentication, request/response envelope, and available endpoints
- [SDKs (Beta)](https://docs.affitor.com/api-reference/sdks): affitor-sdk (browser) + affitor-node (server) reference
- [Agent Integration (Beta)](https://docs.affitor.com/api-reference/agent-integration): .affitor/AGENTS.md + skills.md the CLI generates for AI agents
- [Attribution](https://docs.affitor.com/api-reference/attribution): How Affitor links clicks, signups, and sales for attribution
- [Error Reference](https://docs.affitor.com/api-reference/errors): Error codes and troubleshooting guide

### Tracking Endpoints

- [Track Click](https://docs.affitor.com/api-reference/track-click): Record an affiliate click event when a visitor lands via a referral link
- [Track Lead](https://docs.affitor.com/api-reference/track-lead): Record a signup or lead event that links a customer to an affiliate click
- [Track Sale](https://docs.affitor.com/api-reference/track-sale): Record an attributed sale and create the partner commission from your backend
- [Track Refund](https://docs.affitor.com/api-reference/track-refund): Reverse a commission when a sale is refunded

### Program Endpoints

- [Get Tracking Status](https://docs.affitor.com/api-reference/status): Returns integration step statuses and Stripe connection info for a program
- [List Tracking Events](https://docs.affitor.com/api-reference/events): Retrieve paginated, merged tracking events (clicks, leads, conversions) for a program
- [GET /tracking/performance/:programId](https://docs.affitor.com/api-reference/performance): Retrieve daily performance aggregates and period-over-period trend metrics

### Integrations

- [Next.js](https://docs.affitor.com/api-reference/integrations/nextjs): Add Affitor tracking to a Next.js App Router app — clicks, signups, sales (affitor-sdk + Stripe integration)
- [Next.js + Clerk](https://docs.affitor.com/api-reference/integrations/nextjs-clerk): Fire the signup from Clerk auth, with click-id forwarding for server webhooks
- [Next.js + Supabase](https://docs.affitor.com/api-reference/integrations/nextjs-supabase): Fire the signup from Supabase Auth state changes
- [Next.js + NextAuth](https://docs.affitor.com/api-reference/integrations/nextjs-nextauth): Fire the signup from NextAuth events.createUser, forwarding the click id
- [Stripe integration](https://docs.affitor.com/api-reference/integrations/stripe): Attach affitor_click_id / affitor_customer_key / program_id metadata to Checkout Sessions; Affitor reads webhooks (one-time + subscription)
- [Node / Express](https://docs.affitor.com/api-reference/integrations/node-express): Server-side tracking with affitor-node (trackLead / trackSale / trackRefund) + raw API

## Support

- [Support](https://docs.affitor.com/support): Fastest self-serve path and when to contact the team
- [FAQ](https://docs.affitor.com/faq): Common questions about pricing, tracking, commissions, and integration flows
- [Contact](https://docs.affitor.com/support/contact): Get in touch with the Affitor team for support, partnerships, or press inquiries
- [System Status](https://docs.affitor.com/support/status): Check current public status guidance and what to do if you suspect an issue
- [Glossary](https://docs.affitor.com/support/glossary): Key terms used across Affitor docs

## SDKs

### affitor-sdk

Browser-side click tracking SDK. Install via npm or use the script tag.

**npm install:**

```bash
npm i affitor-sdk
```

**React usage:**

```tsx
// app/providers.tsx — client component
'use client';
import { useEffect } from 'react';
import { init } from 'affitor-sdk';
export function AffitorInit() {
  useEffect(() => { init({ programId: YOUR_PROGRAM_ID }); }, []);
  return null;
}
// render <AffitorInit /> once in app/layout.tsx
```

**Script tag:**

```html
<script src="https://api.affitor.com/js/affitor-tracker.js"
  data-affitor-program-id="YOUR_PROGRAM_ID">
</script>
```

**Browser signup helper:**

```javascript
signup(customerKey, email) // SDK
// or
await window.affitor.signup(userId, userEmail);
```

### affitor-node

Server-side Node.js SDK for lead and sale tracking from your backend. See the [API Reference](https://docs.affitor.com/api-reference/overview) for direct API usage.

## AI Agent Integration

The Affitor CLI (`npx affitor init`) generates two files in your project:

- `.affitor/AGENTS.md` — machine-readable integration spec for AI coding agents (Claude, Cursor, Copilot, etc.). Describes the tracking contract, API endpoints, and identifier consistency rules so agents can implement Affitor correctly without hallucinating.
- `.affitor/skills.md` — step-by-step skill definitions for common integration tasks (add click tracking, add signup tracking, add sale tracking, test integration).

These files are designed to be consumed by AI agents performing automated integration. They are generated fresh by `npx affitor init` and stay in sync with your program configuration.

## Quick Integration

### Integration (3 steps)

**Step 1 — Click tracking**: Add the tracker script to your site `<head>`:

```html
<script src="https://api.affitor.com/js/affitor-tracker.js"
  data-affitor-program-id="YOUR_PROGRAM_ID">
</script>
```

Or install via npm: `npm i affitor-sdk`

```tsx
// app/providers.tsx — client component
'use client';
import { useEffect } from 'react';
import { init } from 'affitor-sdk';
export function AffitorInit() {
  useEffect(() => { init({ programId: YOUR_PROGRAM_ID }); }, []);
  return null;
}
// render <AffitorInit /> once in app/layout.tsx
```

**Step 2 — Signup tracking**: Call after user registration succeeds:

```javascript
// Browser-side
await window.affitor.signup(userId, userEmail);
```

```bash
# Server-side
POST https://api.affitor.com/api/v1/track/lead
Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY
Content-Type: application/json

{"customer_key": "user_123", "email": "user@example.com"}
```

**Step 3 — Sale tracking** (Server-side tracking vs Stripe integration):

Option A: Stripe integration (Stripe metadata + webhook):

Option B: Server-side tracking — your backend calls POST /api/v1/track/sale (any payment provider):

```bash
POST https://api.affitor.com/api/v1/track/sale
Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "transaction_id": "txn_unique_id",
  "customer_key": "user_123",
  "amount_cents": 4900,
  "currency": "USD"
}
```

Option C: Stripe metadata (manual):

```javascript
metadata: {
  affitor_click_id: clickId,
  affitor_customer_key: userId,
  program_id: 'YOUR_PROGRAM_ID'
}
// For subscriptions, also set subscription_data.metadata with same fields
```

## Identifier Consistency

Use the same internal user ID everywhere:

| Context | Field Name |
|---|---|
| Browser signup | `customerKey` (1st argument to signup()) |
| Lead API | `customer_key` |
| Server-side tracking | `customer_key` |
| Stripe metadata | `affitor_customer_key` |

## API Reference

| Endpoint | Method | Auth | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `/api/v1/track/click` | POST | none (public) | Record a click. No API key — attribution is proven by the `?aff=` short code inside `affiliate_url`. Normally fired by the browser tracker, not server-side. |
| `/api/v1/track/lead` | POST | Bearer API key (server) or none (browser) | Track signup/lead. Browser mode (`window.affitor.signup`) uses the click cookie; server mode uses the Bearer key. |
| `/api/v1/track/sale` | POST | Bearer API key | Track sale/payment |
| `/api/v1/track/refund` | POST | Bearer API key | Reverse a commission on refund |
| `/api/v1/cli/test-event` | POST | Bearer API key | Fire a synthetic click→lead→sale chain (`{"type":"chain"}`) to verify the pipeline |
| `/api/v1/programs/me/readiness` | GET | Bearer API key | Poll the 5-gate readiness verdict until `integration_verified: true` |
| Base URL: `https://api.affitor.com` (production; uat/staging differ — use the API base you were given) | | | |

**Dashboard session-auth only (NOT key-authed — for the logged-in advertiser UI, not agent integration):**

| Endpoint | Method | Auth | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `/api/tracking/status` | GET | dashboard session auth only | Integration step statuses for the dashboard |
| `/api/tracking/events` | GET | dashboard session auth only | Paginated merged tracking events for the dashboard |
| `/api/tracking/performance/:programId` | GET | dashboard session auth only | Daily performance aggregates for the dashboard |

## Conventions

- All amounts in cents (e.g., $49.00 = 4900)
- `transaction_id` must be unique per sale (duplicate returns 409)
- Test mode: add `"additional_data": {"test_mode": true}` to skip commission creation
- Attribution: first-party cookie, last-click, 60-day default window

## Pricing

- No monthly subscription, no setup fee
- 3.5% platform fee on partner-generated revenue
- $10K fee-free threshold for new programs
